skill.name: mermaid-expert
skill.description: Create Mermaid diagrams for flowcharts, sequences, ERDs, and architectures. Masters syntax for all diagram types and styling. Use PROACTIVELY for visual documentation, system diagrams, or process flows.
skill.summary: Create readable Mermaid diagrams for systems, processes, data models, and timelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as false positives. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting and a Mermaid type list, while the weak cryptography and system reconnaissance labels match ordinary prose. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, network activity, executable scripts, or command execution.

content.value_statement: Teams often need clear diagrams but lose time choosing syntax and layout. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce Mermaid diagrams with suitable structure, labels, and styling.
actual_capabilities: Selects suitable Mermaid diagram types for processes, APIs, data models, journeys, timelines, and architectures.
Creates basic and styled Mermaid versions for the same diagram request.
Applies readability guidance for labels, colors, structure, and diagram density.
Explains complex Mermaid syntax with comments when needed.
Provides rendering guidance, alternatives, accessibility notes, and export recommendations.
limitations: It does not include a renderer or visual preview engine.
It relies on the host assistant to validate Mermaid syntax.
It cannot verify external system architecture without accurate input.
The referenced implementation playbook is not present in this package.

use_cases.title: Document a Product Workflow
use_cases.description: Turn a user journey or decision process into a readable Mermaid flowchart with labels and styling.

use_cases.title: Map API Interactions
use_cases.description: Create a sequence diagram that explains request flow, service boundaries, and response handling.

use_cases.title: Explain a Data Model
use_cases.description: Convert entities, relationships, and cardinality into an ERD for technical documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Flowchart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a process diagram.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid flowchart for this process: [describe the steps]. Include a basic version and a styled version.

prompt_templates.title: Design an API Sequence Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for service interaction documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid sequence diagram for these actors and API calls: [list actors and calls]. Show success and failure paths.

prompt_templates.title: Build an ERD From Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid ERD for these entities and relationships: [paste requirements]. Include cardinality and brief notes on assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Complex Architecture Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for readability and styling improvements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Mermaid architecture diagram for clarity: [paste diagram]. Reduce clutter, improve grouping, and provide an alternative layout.

output_examples.input: A checkout flow with cart review, payment, fraud check, and confirmation.
output_examples.output: A flowchart plan that separates customer actions, payment processing, fraud review, and final order status with a styled variant.

output_examples.input: An API call from a web app through an API gateway to billing and notification services.
output_examples.output: A sequence diagram description showing each participant, request order, success response, and error branch.

output_examples.input: A project plan with discovery, design, implementation, testing, and launch phases.
output_examples.output: A Gantt chart outline with dated phases, dependencies, milestones, and export guidance.

best_practices: Provide exact entities, actors, or steps before requesting the diagram.
Ask for both basic and styled versions when the diagram will be published.
Validate the final Mermaid output in your target renderer.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a large architecture diagram without boundaries or grouping rules.
Do not use decorative styling that makes relationships harder to read.
Do not assume generated diagrams are correct without checking source requirements.

faq.question: What diagram types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, ERDs, Gantt charts, pie charts, journeys, timelines, and related Mermaid types.

faq.question: Does it render diagrams directly?
faq.answer: No. It creates Mermaid content and rendering guidance, but a separate Mermaid renderer is needed for visual preview.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing Mermaid diagram?
faq.answer: Yes. It can simplify structure, improve labels, suggest styling, and propose alternative diagram types.

faq.question: Is it useful for software architecture documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create architecture and network-style diagrams when service boundaries and relationships are provided.

faq.question: Does it require external commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill file contains guidance only and does not define scripts or command execution.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
